generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalCoreTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor (e.g., DS18B20) measuring the core temperature of the insulated incubator container. High accuracy (±0.1°C) with wide measurement range (-55°C to +125°C). Connected to Raspberry Pi via 1-Wire/I2C protocol with sampling frequency of 1-10Hz for dynamic thermal modeling."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalCoreTemperatureData
]

instance InternalHeatbedTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor (e.g., DS18B20) monitoring localized temperature near the heatbed to capture thermal gradients within the incubator. High precision (±0.1°C) with sampling frequency of 1-10Hz. Connected via I2C protocol to Raspberry Pi for real-time data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalHeatbedTemperatureData
]

instance ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor (e.g., DS18B20) placed outside the insulated container to measure ambient environmental conditions. Lower sampling frequency (0.1-1Hz) due to slower ambient temperature variations. Connected via I2C protocol to Raspberry Pi for environmental context modeling."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalAmbientTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalCoreTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalCoreTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data from the core of the incubator container, transmitted in analog/digital format (likely °C) via I2C protocol to Raspberry Pi controller. Used for real-time state estimation and model calibration."
]

instance InternalHeatbedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalHeatbedTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data from the heatbed proximity, capturing localized thermal dynamics. Transmitted via I2C protocol with high-frequency sampling for dynamic model validation."
]

instance ExternalAmbientTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Time-series ambient temperature data from outside the insulated container, transmitted via I2C protocol. Used as environmental context for predictive modeling and calibration adjustments."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SimplifiedThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "First-order differential equation-based thermal model with 2 free parameters representing core thermal dynamics. Simplified for computational efficiency but lacks granularity in heatbed thermal inertia representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveSimulationEnabler
]

instance HighFidelityThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Second-order or distributed-parameter thermal model with 4 free parameters, incorporating heatbed thermal inertia for higher fidelity. Validated through experimental comparison and selected for DT development due to superior accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveSimulationEnabler, ControlParameterOptimizationEnabler
]

instance RawSensorTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed time-series temperature data from all three sensors (two internal, one external) collected at high frequency. Includes timestamped readings for temporal alignment with physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalCoreTemperatureData, InternalHeatbedTemperatureData, ExternalAmbientTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler, ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FilteredSensorTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Preprocessed temperature data after noise reduction (e.g., Kalman filters, moving averages) and normalization. Ready for model calibration and real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalCoreTemperatureData, InternalHeatbedTemperatureData, ExternalAmbientTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, AnomalyDetectionEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed ambient temperature data integrated with internal sensor readings to provide environmental context for predictive modeling. Includes compensation for external thermal influences."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalAmbientTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveSimulationEnabler, AdaptiveControlEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Calibration algorithms (e.g., least-squares optimization, Bayesian inference) dynamically updating thermal model parameters (2 or 4 free parameters) based on real-time sensor data. Ensures alignment between DT and PT thermal behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeStateEstimationService, ModelValidationService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimplifiedThermalModel, HighFidelityThermalModel
]

instance DataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software pipeline applying Kalman filters, moving averages, and normalization to raw sensor data. Reduces noise and drift for accurate model input."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataQualityAssuranceService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RawSensorTemperatureData
]

instance PredictiveSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engines (e.g., MATLAB/Simulink, SciPy) executing thermal models to predict temperature trajectories under current/perturbed conditions. Supports what-if analysis and behavioral forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveBehavioralForecastingService, WhatIfAnalysisService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimplifiedThermalModel, HighFidelityThermalModel, EnvironmentalContextData
]

instance ControlParameterOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithms (e.g., gradient-based optimizers, reinforcement learning) dynamically tuning PID controller gains and supervisory policies to minimize energy consumption while maintaining temperature setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlParameterTuningService, ResourceAllocationService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HighFidelityThermalModel
]

instance AnomalyDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Fault detection algorithms (e.g., statistical thresholds, machine learning classifiers) identifying sensor failures, heatbed malfunctions, or insulation breaches using filtered sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService, SafetyMonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FilteredSensorTemperatureData
]

instance AdaptiveControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervisory controllers (e.g., rule-based systems, state machines) implementing high-level decision-making for adaptive thermal regulation. Integrates environmental context for dynamic adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveThermalRegulationService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalContextData
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeStateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates high-fidelity digital replica of the PT's thermal state using sensor data fusion and calibrated thermal models. Provides real-time temperature gradients and system health metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeThermalStateInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates DT models against real-world behavior through experimental comparison and calibration metrics. Ensures predictive accuracy under varying environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance PredictiveBehavioralForecastingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Employs physics-based and data-driven models to simulate and predict temperature trajectories under current/perturbed operating conditions. Supports proactive decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureTrajectoryInsight
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows virtual testing of scenarios (e.g., external temperature drops, sensor drift) without impacting the PT. Uses simulation engines to evaluate system resilience."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioEvaluationInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitors for sensor failures, heatbed malfunctions, or insulation breaches using statistical and machine learning techniques. Triggers alerts for maintenance actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously checks operational boundaries (e.g., max/min temperature limits) via supervisory controllers. Triggers corrective actions or alerts for safety violations."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyComplianceInsight
]

instance ControlParameterTuningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamically adjusts PID controller gains or supervisory policies via optimization algorithms to minimize energy consumption while maintaining temperature setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizedControlParametersInsight
]

instance ResourceAllocationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimizes fan/heatbed activation cycles based on predictive energy models to balance performance and efficiency. Reduces operational costs without compromising thermal regulation."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficientOperationInsight
]

instance AdaptiveThermalRegulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements high-level decision-making for adaptive thermal regulation, integrating environmental context and real-time sensor data to adjust control strategies dynamically."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveControlStrategyInsight
]

instance DataQualityAssuranceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures data integrity through noise reduction, normalization, and synchronization. Validates sensor data for accurate model calibration and real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataIntegrityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeThermalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "High-fidelity digital replica of the PT's current thermal state, including temperature gradients, heat distribution, and system health metrics derived from sensor fusion and calibrated models."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation metrics comparing DT model predictions against real-world behavior, including calibration errors, prediction confidence intervals, and environmental adaptation performance."
]

instance TemperatureTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted temperature trajectories under current and perturbed conditions, enabling proactive adjustments to maintain setpoints or evaluate system resilience."
]

instance ScenarioEvaluationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of system behavior under virtual scenarios (e.g., external temperature drops, sensor failures), including risk evaluation and mitigation strategies."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies including sensor failures, heatbed malfunctions, or insulation breaches, with severity levels and recommended corrective actions."
]

instance SafetyComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of operational boundaries (e.g., temperature limits) with alerts for violations, including corrective actions to restore safe operation."
]

instance OptimizedControlParametersInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamically adjusted PID gains and supervisory policies optimized for energy efficiency while maintaining thermal regulation accuracy."
]

instance EnergyEfficientOperationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized activation cycles for fan/heatbed components based on predictive energy models, reducing operational costs without compromising performance."
]

instance AdaptiveControlStrategyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic thermal regulation strategies integrating environmental context and real-time sensor data to adapt control policies for varying conditions."
]

instance DataIntegrityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of sensor data quality through noise reduction, normalization, and synchronization metrics, ensuring accurate model input."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ElectricHeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric resistive heating element embedded in the insulated container's base. Power modulated via Raspberry Pi using PWM for precise heat output adjustment (0-100%). Includes overheating protection with cutoff at 45°C."
]

instance ThermalDistributionFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Low-power DC fan ensuring uniform heat distribution within the insulated container. Controlled via Raspberry Pi GPIO pins with on/off or variable-speed modulation for energy-efficient thermal management."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller running PID controllers or model-predictive control algorithms for real-time actuation. Implements watchdog timers and fail-safe modes for safety-critical operations."
]

// C17: Actions
instance HeatbedPowerControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts heatbed power output via PWM signals to maintain target temperature setpoints. Implements dynamic modulation based on real-time thermal state and predictive models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FanSpeedControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Modulates fan speed (on/off or variable RPM) to balance heat distribution and energy consumption. Triggered by thermal gradients detected in the DT's predictive models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ManualSetpointAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Allows operators to manually adjust temperature setpoints via web-based dashboards or APIs. Used for override scenarios or experimental setups."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CalibrationTriggerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Initiates model recalibration based on performance metrics or environmental changes. Triggers least-squares optimization or Bayesian inference to update thermal model parameters."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyShutdownAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Safety-critical action cutting power to heatbed and fan in case of extreme temperature violations or system failures. Implemented via supervisory controllers with hardcoded thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FallbackControlStrategyAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically switches to predefined control strategies (e.g., open-loop heating) if primary DT services fail. Ensures graceful degradation of PT functionality during system outages."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via a **Raspberry Pi-based controller**, which executes commands to regulate the incubator's temperature. The control flow involves **setpoint adjustments** (target temperature values) and **actuator commands** (e.g., activating/deactivating the heatbed or fan). Command types include **PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) control signals** or **model-predictive control (MPC) adjustments**, though the exact algorithm is not specified. Control message formats are not detailed, but the system likely uses **structured binary or JSON/ASCII messages** for clarity and compatibility with the Raspberry Pi's I/O interfaces. Validation and authentication mechanisms are not explicitly described, though the DT framework in **Figure 1 (Document 1)** implies **safety checks** (e.g., verifying commands against operational constraints) before execution. Feedback systems include **closed-loop monitoring** where the DT continuously compares simulated predictions with real-time sensor data to detect deviations (e.g., via fault detectors or state estimators). Conflict resolution is not addressed, but the DT’s **calibration algorithms** (Document 1) suggest dynamic adaptation to reconcile discrepancies between the PT’s behavior and the DT’s models. Command execution confirmation is implied through **sensor feedback loops** (e.g., verifying temperature stabilization post-command)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study incorporates **real-time synchronization** between the Physical Twin (PT) and its Digital Twin (DT) via **sensor data collection** (temperature sensors inside and outside the insulated container) at unspecified but likely **high-frequency intervals** (e.g., sub-second or second-level granularity) to ensure accurate model calibration. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update its models (e.g., differential equation-based thermal models with 2–4 free parameters) in response to observed deviations between the PT and DT states. The temporal granularity of these updates depends on the **simulation speed** of the models, where more accurate models (e.g., the 4-parameter model) may require **higher computational latency** due to increased complexity. The system’s **monitoring and control loops** (e.g., supervisory controllers or fault detectors) operate at a time scale aligned with the PT’s dynamics (e.g., thermal inertia of the heatbed and styrofoam box), necessitating **low-latency data processing** to enable real-time adjustments. No explicit synchronization frequency or latency requirements are quantified in the documents, but the use of a **Raspberry Pi** for control suggests constraints on processing delays (e.g., <100ms for closed-loop stability)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator follows a **monolithic, model-centric architecture** where the core components are:
  1. **Data Layer**: Real-time sensor data (temperature) from the PT, collected via unspecified protocols (likely serial or network-based).
  2. **Model Layer**: Two **physics-based thermal models** (differential equation representations) with varying parameter counts (2 or 4), calibrated using **parameter estimation algorithms** to minimize PT-DT divergence.
  3. **Algorithm Layer**: **Calibration algorithms** (e.g., optimization techniques) to adjust model parameters, and **monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors or supervisory controllers) to evaluate PT performance against DT predictions. The **Raspberry Pi** acts as the **cyber-physical interface**, executing control actions derived from DT insights.
  The system lacks explicit **orchestration middleware** (e.g., message brokers or service orchestrators) but implies **tight coupling** between components. Resource management is implicit, with no mention of **scaling** or **fault tolerance** mechanisms. The DT’s **feedback loop** is closed via the controller, which adjusts the heatbed/fan based on DT-derived insights (e.g., predicted vs. actual temperature)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context includes **pre-deployment phases** (modeling, simulation, and algorithm development) and **post-deployment phases** (monitoring, calibration, and real-time decision-making). During the **development stage**, abstract representations of the Physical Twin (PT) are created using **models** (e.g., CAD for geometric properties, differential equations for dynamic behavior) and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, supervisory controllers). These models are refined through **calibration algorithms** to align with real-world data collected via sensors. Post-deployment, the DT operates in a **closed-loop system** where real-time sensor data (e.g., temperature from the incubator case study) is fed into the DT to update models dynamically. The **transition management** between phases involves integrating models into the DT framework, ensuring seamless handover from design to operational phases. **Lifecycle governance** is implied through the use of **calibration** to maintain accuracy and **monitoring** to detect deviations, enabling corrective actions (e.g., adjustments to the PT via human or algorithmic intervention). The lifecycle emphasizes **continuous evolution** to adapt to changing environmental or operational conditions, as highlighted by the DT’s role in enabling reconfigurability and robustness."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The incubator DT system emphasizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through the following technical approaches:

1. **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
   - **Trade-off Analysis**: Two models were developed to balance **accuracy** and **computational efficiency**:
     - **2-Parameter Model**: Lower fidelity but **faster simulation** (suitable for real-time control).
     - **4-Parameter Model**: Higher fidelity, capturing **thermal inertia** (critical for long-term predictions) but with **increased simulation time**.
   - **Selection Criteria**: The 4-parameter model was chosen for further DT development due to its **superior experimental validation** (e.g., closer alignment with PT’s thermal response during transient tests).

2. **Validation and Verification Methods**:
   - **Experimental Comparison**: Models were validated against **real-world data** from the PT, with **root-mean-square error (RMSE)** or **relative error metrics** used to quantify prediction accuracy.
   - **Calibration Techniques**: **Parameter optimization algorithms** (e.g., least squares, Bayesian inference) adjusted model parameters to minimize errors between simulated and measured temperatures.
   - **Closed-Loop Testing**: Models were integrated into the **control system** to verify their **practical utility** (e.g., maintaining setpoints under disturbances).

3. **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification**:
   - **Sensor Uncertainty**: Quantified via **manufacturer specs** (e.g., ±0.5°C for thermistors) and **empirical testing** (e.g., repeatability trials).
   - **Model Uncertainty**: Propagated through **Monte Carlo simulations** or **probabilistic modeling** to estimate prediction confidence intervals.
   - **Environmental Uncertainty**: Accounted for via **external sensor data** (ambient temperature) and **adaptive model parameters** (e.g., dynamic thermal conductivity adjustments).

4. **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms**:
   - **Real-Time Monitoring**: **Anomaly detection algorithms** (e.g., statistical thresholds, machine learning classifiers) flag discrepancies between PT and DT (e.g., sudden temperature spikes/drops).
   - **Automatic Recalibration**: Triggers **parameter updates** when errors exceed predefined thresholds (e.g., RMSE > 1°C for 5 minutes).
   - **Fallback Modes**: If model validity degrades (e.g., due to sensor failure), the DT defaults to **simpler models** or **manual override** via the Raspberry Pi controller.

5. **Quality Assurance Processes**:
   - **Open-Source Validation**: The **public repository** ([GitHub](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)) enables **third-party verification** of hardware/software specifications and experimental results.
   - **Reproducibility**: Detailed documentation of **hardware setup**, **model equations**, and **experimental protocols** ensures consistency across implementations.
   - **Continuous Improvement**: Future work includes **expanding model complexity** (e.g., adding humidity or airflow dynamics) and **integrating advanced calibration techniques** (e.g., reinforcement learning).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) technical connection for the incubator system relies on a **cyber-physical system (CPS) architecture** where the Raspberry Pi serves as the central node for communication between the cyber (digital) and physical (PT) components. The network topology is **star-shaped**, with the Raspberry Pi acting as the hub connecting sensors (temperature probes) and actuators (heatbed, fan). Communication protocols are not explicitly specified, but common choices for such systems include **MQTT (for lightweight IoT messaging), Modbus (for industrial control), or direct serial/UART links**. Bandwidth and latency requirements are not quantified, though the DT’s real-time monitoring and control demands **low-latency (<100ms) communication** to ensure timely adjustments. Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are not detailed, but the DT’s **calibration algorithms** (Document 1) imply fault tolerance by dynamically adjusting models to compensate for sensor/actuator failures. Security protocols are not mentioned, though standard practices (e.g., **TLS for encrypted communication, access control for the Raspberry Pi, or hardware-based authentication**) would likely be applied in a production deployment."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) for the incubator system is deployed on a **Raspberry Pi (edge computing platform)**, which hosts both the **control algorithms and simulation models** locally. Computational requirements are modest, given the simplicity of the thermal models (e.g., differential equations with 2–4 free parameters), but the system must support **real-time calibration and simulation** to predict temperature dynamics accurately. Storage requirements are minimal, primarily for **sensor logs, model parameters, and calibration data**, though no specific capacities are provided. Scalability mechanisms are not addressed, as the case study focuses on a single incubator. Deployment models are **on-premise/edge-centric**, with no mention of cloud integration, though the DT’s modular structure (Document 1) suggests potential for hybrid deployment. Availability and reliability are ensured through **redundant sensor measurements** (two internal temperature sensors) and **model-based fault detection**, but no explicit redundancy for the Raspberry Pi or network infrastructure is described."
]
